stories: U.S. equities: rotation day—banks/industrials up, tech/telecom lag (factor churn, not a clean index tape).
SpaceX “SPCX” extends the melt-up: >50% in three sessions (IPO repricing + options fuel risk-on).
Oil: Hormuz “reopening” narrative isn’t a full all-clear—vol stays bid.
Rates (fresh): U.S. Treasury 20-year auction clears at 4.927%—better digestion than feared.
FX: USD soft near 10-day lows, but USD/JPY remains glued near 160—intervention gap-risk stays live.
Europe: ECB expected to hold—messaging remains “data-dependent” (wages/HICP/transmission).
Crypto: cooling tape—open interest down, liquidations up, ETF flow tone turns mixed.
Insider buy (energy): Expand Energy (EXE) interim CEO buys stock—adds to already-large stake.
Insider buy (micro-cap/CEF signal): Royce Micro-Cap Trust (RMT) president buys 3,600 shares at $14.12.
Gold: central banks repatriate bullion—geopolitics is reshaping reserve behavior.
